pathology.cancer_type: Cutaneous mast cell tumor, Patnaik Grade III (Kiupel high-grade), left hind leg, local recurrence. Deep
pathology.clinical_notes:
ONTARIO VETERINARY DIAGNOSTIC LABORATORY
Surgical Pathology Report — Confidential
50 Stone Road East, Guelph ON N1G 2W1   |   Tel: (519) 824-4120   |   Fax: (519) 824-5930
Patient Name:
Luna
Accession #:
OVDL-2026-04710
Species:
Canine (Canis familiaris)
Date of Biopsy:
April 10, 2026
Breed:
Golden Retriever
Date of Report:
April 14, 2026
Age:
8 years
Referring Vet:
Dr. Sarah Chen, DVM
Weight:
32.4 kg
Clinic:
Bloor West Animal Hospital
Sex:
FS (spayed female)
Owner Location:
Toronto, ON, Canada
CLINICAL HISTORY
Luna presented 6 months ago with a firm, ulcerated mass on the left hind leg, proximal to the tarsal joint.
Fine-needle aspiration suggested mast cell neoplasia. The mass was surgically excised on November 12, 2025,
with histopathology confirming Patnaik Grade II (Kiupel high-grade)

sequencing_labs.name: Vidium Animal Health
sequencing_labs.notes: Canine tumor WES + RNA-seq. Established veterinary oncology sequencing provider.

sequencing_labs.name: ImpriMed
sequencing_labs.notes: AI-guided canine lymphoma diagnostics; expanding to solid tumors.

sequencing_labs.name: Fidocure
sequencing_labs.notes: Canine tumor genomic profiling + drug recommendation platform.

sequencing_labs.name: UC Davis Veterinary Genetics Lab
sequencing_labs.notes: Research-grade sequencing + DLA typing. Academic pricing.

sequencing_labs.name: Cornell Animal Health Diagnostic Center
sequencing_labs.notes: Academic diagnostic center with NGS capabilities.

synthesis_vendors.name: Twist Bioscience
synthesis_vendors.notes: Cheapest DNA template source. mRNA production requires IVT downstream.

synthesis_vendors.name: TriLink BioTechnologies
synthesis_vendors.notes: Gold standard for research-grade IVT mRNA. CleanCap Cap-1 + modified bases standard.

synthesis_vendors.name: Aldevron (Danaher)
synthesis_vendors.notes: GMP-grade. Overkill for a single dog; use for multi-patient trials.

synthesis_vendors.name: Maravai LifeSciences
synthesis_vendors.notes: Parent company of TriLink. Good for mid-scale orders.

synthesis_vendors.name: IDT (Integrated DNA Technologies)
synthesis_vendors.notes: Fastest turnaround for DNA templates. Requires downstream IVT.

synthesis_vendors.name: Precision NanoSystems (Cytiva)
synthesis_vendors.notes: Benchtop NanoAssemblr instrument or contract formulation. Royalty-free ionizable lipid.

synthesis_vendors.name: Acuitas Therapeutics
synthesis_vendors.notes: Best-in-class LNP IP. License required; typical for clinical programs, not individual cases.

synthesis_vendors.name: Genevant Sciences
synthesis_vendors.notes: Alternative LNP licensor with clinically-validated formulations.

vet_oncologists.name: Ontario Veterinary College — Mona Campbell Centre
vet_oncologists.notes: Academic oncology service with active immunotherapy trials.

vet_oncologists.name: VCA Canada 404 Veterinary Emergency & Referral Hospital
Flint Animal Cancer Center — Colorado State University
vet_oncologists.notes: Largest academic veterinary cancer center; runs NCI Comparative Oncology trials.

vet_oncologists.name: PennVet Ryan Veterinary Hospital Oncology
vet_oncologists.notes: Mason lab immunotherapy trials (HER2 listeria vaccine for osteosarcoma).

vet_oncologists.name: Animal Medical Center NYC Oncology Service
vet_oncologists.notes: Comprehensive oncology service; clinical trials program.

emails.subject: Request for tumor sequencing services — canine oncology case
emails.body:
Dear Vidium Animal Health team,

I am reaching out on behalf of a canine cancer patient who would benefit from your
tumor sequencing services. Summary below.

Patient: Luna (canine, Cutaneous mast cell tumor, Patnaik Grade III (Kiupel high-grade), left hind leg, local recurrence. Deep). Mutations found: 10. Neoantigen candidates: 15. Top candidate: ALMSELEVL from mutation KIT K642E (predicted affinity 1.64 nM).

We are specifically interested in:
- Whole-exome sequencing (WES) of the tumor biopsy (FFPE or fresh-frozen)
- Matched normal sequencing (buccal swab or blood)
- RNA-seq for expression-level validation of candidate neoantigens
- Somatic variant calling with SnpEff/VEP annotation

Could you please share:
1. Pricing for research-grade and (if available) CLIA-certified tiers
2. Typical turnaround time from sample receipt to annotated VCF
3. Sample shipping requirements (cold chain, formalin, etc.)

Time is a factor — we'd appreciate any expedited options you can offer.

Thank you,
NeoVax Treatment Coordinator

emails.subject: mRNA synthesis request — canine personalized vaccine
emails.body:
Dear TriLink BioTechnologies team,

We would like to place a custom mRNA synthesis order for a veterinary compassionate-use
cancer vaccine application. Details:

Patient: Luna (canine, Cutaneous mast cell tumor, Patnaik Grade III (Kiupel high-grade), left hind leg, local recurrence. Deep). Mutations found: 10. Neoantigen candidates: 15. Top candidate: ALMSELEVL from mutation KIT K642E (predicted affinity 1.64 nM).

Construct specifications (FASTA attached):
- Research-grade IVT mRNA
- Modified bases: N1-methylpseudouridine (N1Me-Ψ)
- 5' cap: Cap-1 (ideally co-transcriptional / CleanCap)
- Poly-A tail: 120 nt
- Quantity: 500 µg (sufficient for prime + boost dosing in a 30 kg patient)

Could you please share:
1. Total pricing including QC (RIN, endotoxin, sterility, particle size)
2. Lead time from order confirmation to delivery
3. Any recommendations on buffer/lyophilization for shipment

Thank you,
NeoVax Treatment Coordinator

emails.recipient_name: Ontario Veterinary College — Mona Campbell Centre
emails.subject: Consultation referral — canine oncology case
emails.body:
Dear Dr. Ontario Veterinary College — Mona Campbell Centre,

I am writing to request a consultation for a canine cancer patient whose owner is
exploring experimental immunotherapy options after exhausting standard of care.

Patient: Luna (canine, Cutaneous mast cell tumor, Patnaik Grade III (Kiupel high-grade), left hind leg, local recurrence. Deep). Mutations found: 10. Neoantigen candidates: 15. Top candidate: ALMSELEVL from mutation KIT K642E (predicted affinity 1.64 nM).

The owner has agreed to consider a compassionate-use personalized neoantigen vaccine.
We have generated candidate epitope designs and would like your expert opinion on:
1. Feasibility given the patient's current condition
2. Adjunct therapy recommendations (checkpoint inhibitors, tyrosine kinase inhibitors)
3. Monitoring strategy (imaging schedule, bloodwork, ELISpot if available)
4. Appropriate informed-consent framework

Would you have 30 minutes for a case review in the next 1-2 weeks? We can provide
the full computational case file in advance.

Thank you for considering this referral.
Best regards,
NeoVax Treatment Coordinator

emails.subject: Pre-submission inquiry — compassionate use mRNA vaccine
emails.body:
Dear Ethics Committee,

I am writing to initiate a pre-submission consultation for a compassionate-use
application involving a personalized mRNA cancer vaccine in a terminal canine patient.

Patient: Luna (canine, Cutaneous mast cell tumor, Patnaik Grade III (Kiupel high-grade), left hind leg, local recurrence. Deep). Mutations found: 10. Neoantigen candidates: 15. Top candidate: ALMSELEVL from mutation KIT K642E (predicted affinity 1.64 nM).

Key justifications:
1. Standard-of-care options have been exhausted (surgery + chemotherapy completed)
2. Tumor biology (KIT exon 11 + 17 co-mutations) predicts poor response to
   remaining approved agents (toceranib)
3. Computational evidence (structural docking, DLA binding prediction) supports
   the rationale for this specific construct
4. Risk/benefit analysis favors the proposed intervention given terminal prognosis

I would welcome guidance on:
- Required pre-submission documentation
- Informed consent template suitable for a veterinary research context
- Any FDA CVM INAD steps we should initiate in parallel

Could we schedule a pre-submission meeting at the committee's convenience?

Respectfully,
NeoVax Treatment Coordinator

timeline.title: Tumor biopsy shipped
timeline.description: Overnight to sequencing lab with cold-chain packaging

timeline.title: WES + RNA-seq complete
timeline.description: Raw data returned from lab; pipeline re-runs with patient-specific VCF

timeline.title: DLA typing returned
timeline.description: Patient's DLA alleles confirmed; top candidates re-scored with real alleles

timeline.title: Candidate review + synthesis order placed
timeline.description: Vet oncologist signs off on top 15 epitopes; mRNA ordered

timeline.title: mRNA received + LNP formulation
timeline.description: QC: RNA integrity, endotoxin, sterility, particle size

timeline.title: Second vaccination (boost)
timeline.description: Intramuscular boost; ELISpot + TCR-seq if available

timeline.title: Response imaging
timeline.description: CT or MRI to measure tumor response vs baseline

timeline.title: ctDNA re-sequencing (optional)
timeline.description: Check for subclonal escape; trigger booster design if needed

plain_english:
Hi — here's what we found for Luna.

The tumor has several DNA typos (mutations). These typos change tiny parts of proteins inside the cancer cells and make them look different from healthy cells. The most important one we found is KIT K642E.

We identified 15 strong candidate fragments from these mutations. A personalized vaccine made from these fragments would teach Luna's immune system to recognize and attack cells carrying them — while leaving healthy cells alone.

Next steps: confirm the plan with a veterinary oncologist, order the mRNA, formulate it into a lipid nanoparticle, and schedule the first injection. The whole process takes about 5-6 weeks.

Honest expectations: personalized cancer vaccines are promising but not guaranteed. Current data (human trials) shows roughly 40-50% of patients see meaningful tumor response. This is best used alongside surgery or standard chemo, not instead of it.

This is a proposed plan. Any treatment decision should be made with a licensed veterinary oncologist who has examined Luna in person.
